behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM generation quality (context-grounded vs generic wording). A mocked or deterministic generator would hide the real failure mode, which is model output relevance under prompt/context variability.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real draft-generation model on representative real email threads and assert the draft contains thread-specific references (for example, named issues/questions from the source thread) above a defined relevance threshold; fail if outputs are generic across distinct threads.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: This is a claim-classification quality behavior (detect contradiction vs KB). If the checker uses an LLM/ML judgment, mocks can pass while real contradiction detection fails; the failure boundary is model inference quality, not just module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Feed real drafted claims plus KB entries containing known contradictions into the production fact-check path and assert contradiction flags are raised for the contradictory claims and not for aligned controls.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on model output under sparse-context conditions (abstain/disclaimer vs hallucination). Integration wiring matters, but the critical real failure is generative behavior that mocks cannot reveal.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real draft generation with retrieval returning no relevant KB facts and assert the output includes the required disclaimer language and avoids unsupported factual assertions.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears at module handoff/state boundaries (approval state, send trigger, transport adapter). A unit test of a single function is insufficient; no real LLM or external OS/network dependency is required for minimum coverage.
behaviors.test_description: Wire workflow state manager + approval UI/API handler + send service in-process; verify send attempts before approval are blocked and only transition to sent after an explicit approval event.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires multiple agentic steps in sequence (generation + factual check), plus orchestration across the pipeline. Single agentic or integration tests can miss cross-step failures and state propagation issues.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end scenario with a real email thread through production orchestration: ingest thread, generate draft with real model, run real fact-check, present approval gate, and confirm no send occurs before approval while all intermediate artifacts are produced correctly.
